name: Wadel, C. Almklov
high school: State School for the Blind, of Gary, S. D.
motto: "My mind to me a kingdom is."
description: Almklov joined the bunch of Freshmen headed for U. N. D. in the fall of '05, after graduating from the State School for the Blind, of Gary, S. D. Ever since he has been an enthusiastic "naught niner," although this year he spends most of his time at the law school.

name: Philana, Capston
motto: "In maiden meditation, fancy free."
description: Miss Capston is a product of St. Thomas high school, and later she attended Hamline University, but, with commendable wisdom, she decided that there was no place like Teachers College, U. N. D., so here she is.

"In maiden meditation, fancy free."

name: William, Henry Clark
motto: "A toiler, a worker among men."
description: Dad has been hanging around these halls so long that lots of people think he should have been elected president. He is interested in all that goes on, and takes delight in stirring the pot himself, once in a while. His greatest weakness is for politics, and he frequently waxes eloquent over "how we skinned Bryan."

name: Alfred, Dean
motto: "A man of parts."
description: Alf hails from Minto. He came here long ago, in 1902, and commenced as a Prep. He is now assiduously cutting up "stiffs" with an eye to future fame in the doctor's profession, where he can put to use the splendid nerve he has so often displayed on the football field.

name: Harry, E. Dickinson
motto: "A fellow that hath two gowns, and everything handsome about him."
description: Dick got the habit in his early youth and has never been able to break his connections with the old U. N. D. He has been in love oftener and harder than any man in school, and no doubt the worst is not yet.

name: Henry, Mabel Georgiana
motto: "An open hearted maiden, true and pure."
description: Mabel has been gathering knowledge for four years on how to teach the young idea to shoot. We recommend her for some place where the Methodist minister is old and ugly, because she is a killer.
text:
"An open hearted maiden, true and pure."

name: Hermann, Halldora
motto: "Would there were more like her."
description: Dora kicks up more dust around here than five ordinary girls, but it's always good, clean, sanitary dust. Among other things, Dora is a politician, an athlete, a society girl, and a good student.
text:
"Would there were more like her."

name: Holmes, Frank B.
motto: "Stand back there! Make room for a man."
description: Frank is the champion middleweight of the class and is always ready to defend his title against all comers. He has the distinction of being the only Senior Mechanical Engineer.
text:
"Stand back there! Make room for a man."

name: Honett, Mary Alma
motto: "I am not only witty in myself, but the cause that wit is in other men."
description: Alma has made her home at the N.D.U. for, lo, these many years, having taken her preparatory, as well as her college work, here. She seems to be a good manager, because she gets her work done without letting it interfere with her enjoyment of college life.

name: Hoskins, Brooks
motto: "A hungry, lean-faced villain, a mere anatomy."
description: It was in the fall of 1903 that Shadow first dropped in and began to skip classes. He is one of those gay dogs that you hear so much about,—not much for looks, but a great noise, and always strictly business. He has chosen for his thesis the momentous subject, "What shall we do between midnight and bedtime?" and is doing a lot of original research work.

name: Thomas, G. Johnson
motto: "To be a well favored man is a gift."
description: Tom's the postmaster, so everybody knows him; as president of the Athletic Association, everybody suspects him; he is a crack end, track man, and basketball player, for which he is admired; he is the strong man of the class, for which he is respected.

name: Charles, Oliver Lee
motto: "One man in his time plays many parts."
description: After attending Valparaiso College, Ind., Charlie joined the Freshman bunch in '05, and has been making his presence felt ever since. He gets his chief joy out of life in trying to improve the condition of his fellow men. The Student Council is Charlie's latest venture.

name: Hazel, Loynes
motto: "My master's degree and I."
description: Judging from the number of courses she has taken in history, her interests are centered in men of the past, rather than those of the present. She has seen fit to take unto herself more "A's" than the rest of us, consequently she has finished her B. A. work.

name: Cecil, C. Mahon
motto: "Though I look old, yet am I strong."
description: "Grandpa" loves to sit around in the evenings, in his old Morris chair, his feet to the fire and his pipe going, and listen to the little foolishnesses of the "Preps," and recall the days when he too thought that the chief end of a college life was a chase after marks. He's a real Foxy Grandpa.

name: Guy, R. McKay
motto: "Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise."
description: He's a quiet sort of a "Guy," but when you look into those eyes so blue—Oh my! Don't think for a minute his name depicts him—far from it—he's one of those all round people we hear about, having dipped into athletics, literature, and society.

name: McLean, Agnes
motto: "There's a language in her eye, her cheek, her lip."
description: Hannah. Agnes is another of our young ladies who has won fame with her pen and ink sketches. She is one of the most popular girls in school,—popular with the other girls, admired by the faculty, and loved by all the boys because—well, she has a little way of her own of smiling into your eyes.

name: Murchie, Mayme H.
description: Hannah. She seems to have impressed her constituents with a sense of her executive ability—however, no one objects to being run by Mayme, for while she's doing it she's making friends. She accomplishes more in less time than ordinary people, and says less about it.

name: Nelson, Norris H.
motto: "He is a gentleman on whom I build an absolute trust."
description: Finley. As usual, industry has its reward. Norris put in a couple of years of prephood showing the athletic director that he was some basketball player, and now he is captain, and the best guard in the business. He also has a few other stunts that he pulls off upon occasion, just to show that he is "there" in other lines.

name: Nesdahl, Elsie
motto: "She is herself of best things the collection."
description: Miss Nesdahl has taken about all the Latin the University has in stock; she has delved as far into the mysteries of mathematics as girls are permitted to, and her familiarity with German is simply shocking.

name: Netcher, Arthur L.
motto: "He shall stand before kings."
description: "Art" could justly lay claim to being one of the best "all round men" in the Senior class. He has won his "N.D." in football, baseball, and basketball, is a crack debater, won the Freshman Oratorical contest in '05, won the Main declamation contest last spring, and was chosen in '08 to represent the Civics Club at the National Intercollegiate Civics League Convention in New York.

name: Oftedal, Sverre
motto: "Music is the medicine of the mind."
description: Doc is one of the latest additions to our class, having come here from the University of Minnesota, but he is already a friend and brother to all of us, and a soul in tune with his surroundings. His violin has made him a welcome addition to the orchestra. His medicine is easy to take.

name: Olsgaard, Constance
motto: "'Tis often constancy to change the mind."
description: We know not whether it was a desire to test the educational value of travel or the travelling value of an education that caused Miss Olsgaard to leave the U. of N. D. for another institution; for Minnesota has also contributed to her education. But she travelled back to the institution of her first choice this year; so that "squares" it.

name: Olson, Mabel Beatrice
motto: "The dauntless spirit of resolution."
description: One of our more recent classmates, who joined us in '06. She makes her presence felt in all college activities, is an orator of no mean ability, a first class student, who doesn't find it necessary to bluff, and an all-round college girl.

name: Parizek, Ella C.
description: Another of that fair city's products—and a mighty good one at that. She too, has shown an unusual affinity for "A's," for has she not already earned her Bachelor's degree, and commenced her Master's work? She hasn't caused much excitement since she joined us, but, like the quiet stream, her influence is deep and lasting.

name: Reading, Arthur K.
motto: "There is a tide in the affairs of men Which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune."
description: Reading noticed in the paper last fall that there was a good bunch about to graduate in North Dakota, so he left his Pennsylvania home to get on the band wagon. He has not unbosomed himself to any great extent in regard to his past, but he started the year right by making a strong bid for the basketball team, and winning a place on the free-for-all.

name: Allan, V. Ritchie
motto: "If music be the food of love, play on."
description: Allan is one of those heartless youths who can see no good thing in Davis Hall,—nay, verily, nor in Macnie! Doubtless this is no compliment to the dormitory girls, but then, as I mentioned before, Allan is heartless, thanks to the little god who blinded him to the charms of all save one.

name: Snell, Frank Atwood Tabor
motto: "He is a very valiant trencherman; he hath an excellent stomach."
description: Frank has a brain in which the bump of organization is abnormally developed, and, as a result, he has established for himself an enviable reputation as a manager, especially as football manager last fall. He is also a debater in the heavyweight class, a social light, glee club soloist for six years, and all round good fellow.

name: Sprague, Maude Carlotta
motto: "To teach or to marry, that is the question."
description: Maude works a little, plays a little, sleeps some, eats a lot, and laughs always, and there you have her philosophy of life. She absolutely refuses to worry, and when there is no one around to amuse, she amuses herself. She is her own best friend.

name: St. Amour, James A.
motto: "What's in a name?"
description: In spite of his amorous cognomen, Jimmie shouts from the hill tops that he has never been in love,—and expects us to believe him!
text:
"What's in a name?"

name: Taylor, Ernest M.
motto: "It takes nine tailors to make a man."
description: He is an earnest man who does more hard work than anybody else in the class. Add to this that confidential way that he has of getting around his instructors, and the result is a combination that is the dread enemy of cons and flunks.

name: Herbert, E. Treichler
motto: "Words are not the only things."
description: Herbert is one of the crack engineers of the institution, but alas! he went skating on the coulee one morning, fell in love with a sorority girl, and no more A's now come his way. He was such a nice boy, too. Isn't it a shame!

name: W., P. Twenge
motto: "Honesty is the best policy."
description: An orderly young man who posts a schedule of his daily doings and then lives up to it. He has established quite a name for himself as a debater and orator, and will undoubtedly make good as the defender of the down-trodden.

name: Warmer, George Acheson
motto: "A proper man as one shall see in a summer's day."
description: One of those steady, dependable fellows who fill a most important niche in University life, yet so quietly that their real worth is not recognized until they are gone. George is a hard worker and a good talker, and will undoubtedly be a success.

name: Wells, Edgar Herbert
motto: "There were courage and grace in his beautiful face."
description: It is quite generally conceded that Ed. is the handsomest man in school, and we are all willing to admit it, in view of the fact that Cupid has already put him out of the running, and there is small danger of his spoiling anyone else's stand-in.

name: Westergaard, Harold W.
motto: "To be two years in love exceedeth a college education."
description: "Westy" is a product of Valley City Normal, but his chief claim to distinction comes from his position behind the book-store counter and his affinity for dark eyed maidens. It is said that Westy's chief aim in life is to secure a charming helpmate.

name: Bennett, Leah Ames
motto: "It likes me ill, to herd with men."
description: Mrs. Bennett came originally from Iowa, and now lives in Grand Forks. She is one of two of the gentler sex enrolled in the Law School.
text:
"It likes me ill, to herd with men."

name: Bagan, Bernard J.
motto: "I could love a 'Merry Widow.'"
description: Destiny has marked him for her own and nothing short of the Supreme Bench will ever satisfy him.
text:
"I could love a 'Merry Widow.'"
Destiny has marked him for her own and nothing short of the Supreme Bench will ever satisfy him.

name: Boe, Alfred S.
motto: "Where have we seen that face before?"
description: After wandering through the Normal at Moorhead, and learning bean talk at Emerson College in Boston, he finally settled at Ruso, N. D.
text:
"Where have we seen that face before?"
After wandering through the Normal at Moorhead, and learning bean talk at Emerson College in Boston, he finally settled at Ruso, N. D.

name: Chittick, William
motto: "The cold neutrality of an impartial judge."
description: As unfathomable as a "non-navigable" stream could possibly be, he can be depended upon to deliver the goods. "Judge" Chittick, the learned ruler of Kangaroo court.
text:
"The cold neutrality of an impartial judge."

name: Clausen, Gabriel J.
motto: "Nature has built him several stories high."
description: He doesn't carry a trumpet, like his namesake, but if you ask him he'll quietly tell you that his present home is at Dalton, Minn., and that he doesn't care who knows his motto is "onward and upward."

name: Colborn, Geo. O.
motto: "And yet 'there are maidens withering on the stalk.'"
description: Like the majority of young men born in Grafton, the class historian has great difficulty in keeping away from the ladies. As an '06 Normal he was surrounded by eighteen, and he has lately added to his accomplishments by tuning his heartstrings to play the Symphony in 14 Flat, (Dinnie Block).

name: Conmy, Edward T.
motto: "He owns a dog."
description: The worthy president is rather reticent about his past, as he is trying to live down the fact that the first three years of his academic course were not of the co-educational variety. He has since fought a good fight, he has finished the course, he has kept the Faith.

name: Denholm, Frank A.
motto: "A man who minds his own business."
description: This son of the soil, who carries nearly all of the homely virtues about on his face, and is always highly patriotic in his dress, started life at Odell, Illinois, in 1882. His greatest ambition is to be a "poor, but honest lawyer." And there are a host of poor ones now.

name: Dreisbach, Ralph H.
motto: "A fool may now and then be right, perchance."
description: He isn't from Missouri, but you'll have to show him just the same. He has attended Yankton Normal and S. D. U., likes short cut coats, and is an authority on boots, shoes, naturalization, and kindred subjects. He "stood up" for the Forum last fall.

name: Edwardson, Kaspar E.
motto: "When the emergency comes he'll cave in the emergency's head."
description: He looked "easy," but his duties as deputy sheriff caused public opinion among the Juniors to take a sudden change, and he is now respected as a quiet citizen from Concordia, the secret of whose success is intense application.

motto: "If I should show all I know, the very first thing, then what?"
description: J. Edward has been accused of spending too much of his time at the N. P. depot, but it seems that he, or some one else, lives up along the line. He is very conscientious, especially in baseball season, and will give away his last cigar—to prevent being caught with it.

name: Halls, Carl B.
motto: "Virtuous and vicious, like the rest of us."
description: C. B. Halls hails from Hills, Minn., but he isn't a mountain climber. He's one of your musical gentlemen, who wears short hair because he has political aspirations. The latter are apt to cause his 25 years of handsomeness to fade before he reaches the little pedestal of the mighty.

motto: "I have done the state some service"—and I know it.
description: Healey is a voice contortionist of no mean ability and has appeared at Aaker's Business College and other leading institutions in the country. He is a good booster and has a lecture entitled, "A father's advice to his son."

name: Herigstad, Omon B.
motto: "He may yet be a man before his mother."
description: While a Junior "Cupid" Herigstad made it his ambition to become a real man, but succeeded only in becoming treasurer of the athletic board and the Y. M. C. A. Consequently he spent most of his Senior year with a patent lawn mower in the Law School attic.

name: Kyllo, Henry L.
motto: "He has outrivalled all his rivals."
description: "Cap" tried to pass off as an Irishman once, but he couldn't tell the difference between green and yellow. He has been the all-Western college catcher for a decade, and doesn't care what the color of her hair is, so that it is the same as his own.

name: Wendell, H. Linwell
motto: "He grew up with the University."
description: The date of his entrance is about 1901. It is ancient history, so to speak, and the memory of man runneth not to the contrary. Money stringencies and the fickleness of women have had no perceptible influence on the correct creasing of his trousers.

name: Lawrence, P. McNulty
motto: "Modesty is not his only merit."
description: He bears the distinction of being the best all around good fellow, with a grace of manner becoming to a prince, and doesn't let it interfere with his work when he represents his society in debate.

name: Alfred, O. Nelson
motto: "Sing me to sleep."
description: He "groped about in a sea of uncertainties" until made Prexy of the class. Now he realizes that Hatton, N. D., isn't such a bad place to be born in after all, but, like Roosevelt, he wants every man to give him a square deal.

name: Geo., I. Rodsater
motto: "A star to which some Dakota beauty should hitch her wagon."
description: The constellations at Luther and the U. of M. were not brilliant enough for this star of the track and the diamond, and so September 22, 1908, found his aerolitic presence at the N. D. U. Four years of athletics at Luther, and a year with the Thulanian Club at Minnesota, made him fit company for us.

name: Bernhart, P. Sandlie
motto: "Don't let him go to the devil, he may not be known."
description: This famous namesake of the well known "Sarah," has a reputation extending from Lakota High School to the Alvareda Cafe in Minneapolis. Lady passengers of the age of 16 or 17 on No. 4 even ask for his whereabouts. "Ain't it awful!" Think of Canby, Minn., being his birthplace.

name: Schmidt, Grover C.
motto: "I was never less alone than when by myself."
description: His greatest aspiration was to live, so he left his home town of Chokio, Minnesota,—who wouldn't?—but, failing to get a "line on" anything at Browns Valley or Hamline, he came to the U., where he has become famous as the discoverer of a thinking man.

name: Schneringer, Fred
motto: "A little on the tags."
description: This custodian of the dust-covered verbiosities of the backwoods judges left his native state of Nebraska just after the great tobacco blight. He reasons as well as if he had never lived in the Bryan commonwealth.

name: Sibell, Frank J.
motto: "I don't believe I've read that one."
description: He originally came from Milwaukee, Wis., which accounts for his desire to get a better knowledge of the law. He's a loyal American barber, red headed and married, but will barber anyone who offers him the required inducement. He occupies the first seat in the "married" row.

name: Selby, Albert E.
motto: "Always of one mind—fresh air."
description: The product of a purely prohibition state, whose head a quarter of a century has left almost in its original bareness, outside, but keen and quick of discernment, inside. His supreme ambition in life is to find two successive hours in one day, well supplied with fresh air.

name: Tighe, W. Jennings
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous."
description: Few have discovered what that first initial stands for. He seems much too retired and unassuming to be known by the name of that familiar and historic landmark of American politics, but he himself admits to being born near "Bill"-dom. He's a bright, intelligent fellow, too.

name: Turcotte, Frank W.
motto: "I love everything that's old"—old wine especially.
description: "Dis 'ere fellow" comes from one of North Dakota's few saintly cities. Some say he is a Frenchman, but he can drop H's with the best of them. The records at St. John show him to be only 20, but he can talk faster, and judge whiskey better than most politicians.

name: Winter, Peter A.
motto: "Lastly comes winter clothed all in frize, chattering his teeth."
description: Despite his frigid name, "Pete" was born in the summer time, and is a warm advocate of equal rights. He digests cases with tropical vengeance. He says he was born August 1, 1884, but a load of learning has bowed his shoulders. He'll be at home after June 15, Canby, Minn.

name: Wenzel, Richard E.
motto: "Sure, he can do it. What is it?"
description: Dick conceived the idea of hunting for degrees while a kid in Berlin, Germany, and it is predicted that before he gets through here he will be the original human thermometer, with enough sheepskins to make himself an overcoat. He came to the U. in 1903, but the U. has been coming to him ever since, so "That's fair enough."

name: Gullickson, John Alfred
motto: "The law of love is even better than the love of law."
description: Originally labelled, "Return to Allamakee," John Alfred has had a troublous time trying to carry away the N. D. Law course. He made an effort in 1904 but just got nicely started when the federal authorities interfered. He has since destroyed the label.

name: Bryan, John Earl
motto: "I wear no man's collar."
description: A republican Irishman direct from Cork. He took in the Perth High School and the Minnesota U. on his way here, and, if he isn't snowbound too often on the Soo, or doesn't laud his namesake too openly, he may some day be a United States senator. Senators have been made from cucumbers.

name: Newton, Carl Abbott, Jr.
motto: "A face like a benediction."
major: Teacher's, A. D. T., Civics Club
description: The "schoolmaster" has been with the class of '10 only a short time. By waiting several years to join us he has reached that delightful period of maturity when, though one of the "boys," he can cut class and successfully work the "grandmother" excuse the next day.

name: Baldwin, John G.
motto: "He carries a convenient frown."
description: "Lucky" Baldwin is a student. He might do a little more work if he were twins, but he wouldn't do any better work. He says that he has been a minor all these years, and he isn't going to let the fact that he is of age keep him from being a miner in the future.

name: Bliss, Vera Mahala
motto: "Bliss beyond compare."
description: Vera is a living denial of the old adage about ignorance being Bliss; and if she had thought that "t'were folly to be wise," just think how much would have been missing from our class basketball team. The serenity of her presence and the sweetness of her voice have been the source of considerable envy among her admirers.

name: Bonzer, Clarence Alexander
motto: "Once I was a poor boy; look at me now."
description: "Chesty" bought his campus ticket in the fall of '05, and immediately made a noise like a football player. Since then he has gained the highest offices, both in athletics and class affairs, being star lineman and captain-elect of the football team, and Prexy of his class. He claims that he'll be a millionaire, provided he can get enough money.

name: Campbell, Lorne Connell
motto: "Like the girls? Well, yes, I rather think so."
description: He made an early reputation in mathematics and the sciences, but in his Sophomore year he was almost killed by a train—of thought passing through his mind. His "Conquest of Devils Lake" is a creditable piece of work. Address all inquiries in care of the Preceptress, Davis Hall.

name: Campbell, Victor Wright
motto: "He talks little but does much."
description: Vic has characterized us all, and written our epitaphs, and, in most cases, he has hit the nail on the head. He hails from Hamilton, N. D., and, ever since coming here, he has been quietly sawing wood. At the national game he is a star. He is an all around man and can always be depended on to do his part and do it well.

name: Conmy, Rose Cathleen
high school: Pembina High School, St. Mary's Academy, Manitoba
motto: "What's in a name? That which we call a Rose By any other name would be as sweet."
description: Before coming to the University to join the class of 1910, Rose attended the Pembina High School, and St. Mary's Academy, Manitoba. Her artistic ability is considered one of the Dacotah's greatest assets.

name: Dean, Cora
high school: East High, Minneapolis, Mayville Normal, U. of Minnesota
motto: "Let us be merry, for life is short."
description: The Dean of Macnie Hall is our class optimist. Her specialty is driving away the blues. Her many talents have made her a good "mixer" and her friends have made her High Chief Fudgemaker of the Mystic Order of S. K. T. She is a candidate for a fudge scholarship to Vassar.

name: Diesem, H. R. S.
motto: "It's very good one can enjoy a little private spouting."
description: He has had many hair-breadth escapes from death, as becomes an orator who practices on second floor Budge. He has a fondness for polysyllabic ratiocination, which is rather embarrassing for a man without a dictionary. In his own words, he is a clasmobranch fish of the order Plagiostomi; in United States, a shark.

name: Erickson, Oscar
motto: "Who's the man that built the Pembina Mountains and put 'em where they are? Erickson! That's me."
description: At first sight one might take Oscar for a student, but at heart he is a Junior, for, if the occasion demands it, he will "cut" a class with as much facility as a Senior would cut a deck of cards. In appearance he is about the size of a real estate man, and he says that already he has land in his pocket, and money out west.

name: Friendberg, Ira C.
motto: "Life is one grand sweet song. Start the music."
description: Ira, the "plentiful" halfback on our class team, is a "disturber of the ivories" and a dispenser of harmony in general. In his early days he gained a popularity much akin to that of Santa Claus by so timing his measles that Christmas exams were excused. Long live Ira!

name: Garnas, Peter
motto: "Out after the coin."
description: "Jim Hill" has been a railway king for several years. He says he likes it because he has to stay here during summer school. He has pulled through three summer schools, unscathed, but hopes to get caught next year. "In summer school," he says, "a man's a man." But Pete's a man the whole year through.

name: Gilroy, William T.
motto: "My life is one dem'd horrid grind."
description: Bill is a plugger. His one recreation is a Sunday morning nap. The rest of the time he studies. His crying fault is a weakness for Hershey's. He is not a woman hater—merely afraid of them.

name: Gladen, Van W.
motto: "Prexy and Thomas and me, but the greatest of these is me."
description: Van says he didn't originate the U., but that it didn't amount to much till he came. As a "hot air" artist and constructive organizer, he ranks with the rankest. The successful manner in which he has managed the business end of the Dacotah would speak well for his future, if necessary, but what's the use?

name: Grinnell, Faith Evelyn
motto: "A squanderer of smiles; a spendthrift of good cheer."
description: And yet she likes Economy. Faith's athletic ability is not confined to basketball, for it is claimed that she holds all the N. D. records for long distance track work. She is the composer of that very popular song entitled: "Who built the Junction so close to the U?"

name: Haskett, Margaret E.
motto: "She scribbleth much."
description: Many people have suspected, and one person has asserted that "The pen is mightier than the sword." Those who oppose this theory are not acquainted with the writings of our Literary Editor, for her satires of the past have laughed many a doubtful contention into a decisive victory for the class. Margaret aspires to become the "presiding genius of die bibliothek," but we are much mistaken if her genius doesn't eventually preside in all the leading bibliotheks in the country.

name: Helmer, Beatrice
motto: "A very gentle and demure little maid."
description: Beatrice didn't join our class until this year, and she likes being a Junior so well that, though she can't remain one, she is going to get her teacher's degree so she can go out and help others to become Juniors.

name: Hermann, Thorhallur
motto: "My lamp and I smoked on."
description: Our "circulating" manager, claims that Edinburg is the town that he is to make famous. He denies that he went east to study for the ministry. After close observation, however, the following might safely be said concerning his religious inclinations: . . . He says that this is the best school of its kind that he has ever attended.

name: Jorgenson, John Axel
motto: "Yes, loving is a painful thrill, And not to love, more painful still."
description: Axel entered the U. a year ahead of us, but he proved to be such a good bluffer that he decided to teach school for a while. The girls say that he is the Boy question of the class. The answer lives down town.

name: Knapp, Harry G.
motto: "What's the use of dreaming, though your name is Knapp?"
description: Knapp, the articulator from Lisbon, started his college career by winning first place in the Freshman Oratorical contest. As a Knight of the Tripod he has made more exposures than the Bureau of Corporations and Link Steffins combined. Though much sought after during leap year, he maintained his condition of single blessedness by frequent use of his "negatives."

name: Kneeshaw, Pauline Elizabeth
motto: "One little hour of joy to me is worth a dull eternity."
description: Pauline has a reputation for "boosting," and her stock expression is, "Let's get something done." Her contributions to "Le Monde Illustre" are highly instructive, especially the one on "What to do with the Chaperone." Her greatest aspiration is to find a locality where they have no college paper, and where even the rubber plant is suppressed.

name: Kvello, Ingvar A.
motto: "Content to let the world wag on as it will."
description: "Kelly" works hard, plays hard, and even loafs hard when he is at it; for instance, he can waste more time in a half an hour than others can in twice the time. He would just as soon jump off Main Building as to look at a girl.

name: Lindstrom, Effie
motto: "Curling locks are rather to be chosen than great riches."
description: Effie has specialized, since coming to the U., in surveying and politics, the former from choice, the latter because she just can't help making friends. Her work in class was so well recognized that it seemed expedient for her to stay out of school during the last state campaign. Rumor has it that John isn't the only Burke elected.

name: Lovell, Harry Bruce
motto: "He is wit's peddler, and he retails his wares."
description: "Rusty," alias "Hank," has gained an enviable reputation for the distance he can go on foot in about 4 minutes and 50 seconds. In view of his Mercury-like propensities, he was made captain of last year's track team. "Rusty" could write a parody on a cook-book that would make Mark Twain sound dry.

name: Lowe, Eleanor E.
motto: "Her vowels flow soft as wine."
description: Her name itself betrays the quality of her voice, which is "sweet and low." She seems to have acquired all the exquisite traits of the "wind of the Western Sea," without being affected with that unlovely one, the tendency to "blow," for Eleanor is too modest for that.

name: LykkEn, Carl
motto: "His action won such reverence sweet, As hid all measure of his feet (feat)."
description: Carl is a strong believer in the adage that silence is gilt-edge stuff, and he owns as much of it to the square inch as any one in the institution. He is a good mixer, however, though sober and staid—staid one evening, it is rumored, till the preceptress came around.

name: Mattson, T. Evelyn
motto: "And the shadow of thy perfect bliss Is the sunshine of ours."
description: Evelyn first started getting her mail at the U. in 1905. We're not going to tell how long her address was New Rockford, her birthplace, because, if we did, you'd be able to figure out that she is not yet out of her 'teens. She is a good student and a good basketball guard.

name: McGuire, Helen
motto: "Of easy temper; naturally good."
description: Helen is, as you may have suspected, half Irish; incidentally, the other half is Irish also, and both halves are always active. Having entered the class as a Freshman while very young, she has had the unique advantage, during her bringing-up, of the most intellectual and dignified associates. Even at that it keeps her busy making believe that she is at least "sweet sixteen."

Even at that it keeps her busy making believe that she is at least "sweet sixteen."

name: Montgomery, Robert Humphrey
motto: "How long, Oh Lord, how long!"
description: "Monte" has been making some of the much advertised scribes of the Elizabethan period turn over in their graves. He would like nothing better than to write poetry like Wordsworth wrote, at the rate of fifty cents a word (seventy-five cents for hard words). About the only place Monte hasn't figured very strongly is on Prexie's want column. Outside of that he is altogether a gentleman.

He was one of the leading men in last year's class drama entitled, "Three Men in a Cab—to say nothing of the Freshman President."

name: Nomland, Julian
motto: "They reckon ill who leave me out."
description: "Whitie" may not be as long as he is short, bodily, but he is deeper than most of them intellectually. His quiet manner has made him popular with the boys, and his blushing countenance has been the thief of many a co-ed's time.

motto: "If off her dignity she should be, The end of the world you'd look to see."
description: Her activities have been along several lines, but her greatest success has been in "Bringing tears to people's eyes," and in talking to the Judges. Nell hasn't taken any track work since the graduation of the class of '08, but then that's easy to answer.

As advertising manager of the Dacotah, he has been "bearding them in their dens" for several months past, and his assortment of business grouches and smiles is now very complete. If you don't start to tell him a story, he will tell you one.

motto: "But ne'er the rose, without the thorn."
description: Edna entered the institution in 1906 to become an enthusiastic member of the enthusiastic class of '10. She is a good student and a jolly companion. Her motto is, "Laugh and the world laughs with you; weep, and the world laughs at you."

name: Pullin, George Mcfarland
motto: "Life is a festival only to the wise."

Even if he accomplishes nothing more, immortality is already secured to him as one of the "Justly famous" book-store "grafters."

description: Miss Telken entered the "U." for the first time in 1905, but dropped out for two years in order to take her place in this year's Junior class. She is a scholar of high rank, and has marked literary ability.

name: Tufft, Harry Earle
motto: "Silence is golden."
description: It may seem paradoxical, but our silent man is the "big noise" in some of his classes. His specialty is "Dutch," and he claims that he gets it without the inspiration of the national beverage that made Milwaukee jealous.

name: Turner, Echo E.
motto: "How sweet the answer Echo makes." —MOORE.
description: Byron says that the world is but an Echo, therefore Echo is all the world. q. e. d. (We won't mention any names.) Echo has done her part in repairing the faults in the harmony of our class and board meetings. She is a collector of autographs, her list of names being as long as any in the circulation department.

name: Torgeson, Theodore A.
motto: "Alexander thought he did it. Look at me."
clubs: Pres. U. N. D. Republican Club
description: When Torgie left Minnesota and set out to get a reputation, he came to the U. of N. D., where they have "reps" to burn. He was undecided as to which one he wanted to connect with, so he annexed several of them. Torgie hopes to become an able lawyer and, after nine months' experience keeping a Dacotah Board at work, his highest aspiration ought to be comparatively "easy money."

She is one of the two lady "engineers" of the class who made surveying so popular last spring. Her topographic map of the campus, showing the cosy-corners, is recommended to all "fussers."

name: Westeen, Clara
motto: "Two lips with bloom and fragrance of a rose."
description: Clara is especially noted for the fact that she has a speaking acquaintance with about five languages; that, when she talks, she talks like a book, and that, like all good books, she shuts up at the proper time. She is taking Law by proxy.

name: Whipple, Archie Osmar
motto: "On their own merits modest men are dumb."
description: Those who know Archie best do not give all the credit for his generous supply of gray matter to the use of proper breakfast food. As a member of last spring's famous "early-bird" surveying team, he not only made the official map of the campus, but he also put Lisbon "on the map."

name: White, H. E.
motto: "I am not in the role of common men."
description: We got our art editor from far off England, where so many bright men have come from. His arrival here at such an early age only goes to prove that the brighter they are the quicker they come. He is a vocalist of considerable renown, having sung tenor before the queen of England—ever thought of singing tenor?

name: White, Harold Francis
motto: "Six days does he labor and do all his work."
description: So who cares if he does visit Macnie Hall on the seventh? Harold comes from Portland, this state. He is a conscientious student, with a bad habit of getting along. His classmates are unable to decide whether he will be a preacher or a politician, but they are unanimous in picking him for a winner.

name: Wold, Albert
motto: "Who knows but this be the skull of a lawyer."
description: Albert blew in from Cummings in '06. Where is Cummings? Why, it's out behind Wold's farm. He has already figured in politics in his district, and he has a card index of every vote in the county. His vigorous handshake and his "Quaker Oat" smile will probably land him in the senate.

name: York, Frederick Russell
motto: "Would he were fatter!"
description: "Dad," ex-president of the class of '10, is not a boarding house ad. His special stunt is that of giving an exact imitation of a student getting all A marks. He is a shark in Mathematics and Physics, and is the formulator of York's Law, that "The amount of gray matter within any given head varies inversely as the hair produced on the epidermal surface."

name: Swenseid, Theodore
motto: "I am one of those gentle ones that will use the devil himself with courtesy."
description: Ted only lacks a few vices to be perfect. On the class baseball team, and in class scraps, he has always been "Johnnie-at-the-rat-hole." If there is any virtue in curly hair and courtesy, he will probably marry the daughter of a "multi."

name: Kent, Frank Wayland
description: "Debs" is so widely known that he needs no picture to refresh the memory. His work while in school has been such as to remove all doubt as to the virtue of a high forehead.

name: Nomland, Jorgen O.
motto: "'Everything goes contrary with me,' said Mrs. Gummidge."
description: "Jargon" is a knocker of the first hammer. As proctor of the third floor, he leads his embattled host against first floor tyranny. Though a Mining Engineer, he delights in the Greek drama and is an admirer of old Omar. He also has a varied collection of "isms" open for public investigation.

motto: "Elmer is quiet, serene and sedate; His smoothness, his knowledge and wisdom are great." —"SOCRATES."
description: He cast his lot with the Fertile High School and St. Olaf College and now he is bound to have an L.L.B. from North Dakota. He is the thirty-second Junior who has stated his highest aspirations as being, "to pass in Contracts."

name: Brownlee, Clarence P.
motto: "To be or not to be."
description: Clarence evidently has executive ability to burn. He has always been prominent along oratorical and literary lines, and has already evinced novelistic propensities that put him in the Jack London class.

name: Blanchard, Horace J.
motto: "I deny the allegations and defy the alligator."
description: "Blanch" is a graduate of Colby High and joined the class the first of the year. He is meek and lowly, but fears not his fellow men, especially Dempsey. He has memorized a little answer to bluff the Dean, "In this case the plaintiff sued the defendant and the higher court reversed the decision."

name: Daily, Mary Agnes
motto: "Sometimes from her eyes We did receive fair speechless messages."
description: Why she came we do not know, nor do we care, so long as she remains among us. Some time ago she concluded that Grand Forks was a much better town than Fargo, where she attended Sacred Heart Academy. She is a graduate of Moorhead Normal and has a fine "stand in" with the Judge.

motto: "Ah! darling tell me the world is wrong! Are you as heartless as they say?"
description: This sprightly form of mother earth's immortal clay first cried for Castoria in Lafayette, Iowa. He attended Lansing High School, but the limitations of that course inflated his ego so that he was compelled to migrate to Dakota.

motto: "The hand that handles the silver and gold is the hand that rules the world."
description: Hanson is a graduate of the schools at Litchfield, Minn. He has had considerable experience along banking lines, and he dreams of banks, bonds, and securities, but he disguises all this by a smiling countenance.

name: King, Harry B.
motto: "Thank the good Lord for a short memory."
description: King prides himself upon being a native born citizen of North Dakota, and he owes his learning chiefly to Pembina environment. He believes in being promptly on time for the first class, even though it necessitates his eating breakfast between classes. His heart's in the Turtle mountains.

name: Gunderson, Sophus Daniel
motto: "Scuse me for living."
description: "Gunder" says that he was born at Albert Lea, Minn., and, as he didn't care much for the place, they shipped him off to Annapolis, where he attended the United States Naval Academy. His custom is to always take a front seat in the "Land of Nod." He aspires to become a great corporation counsel.

name: Fosness, Alfred Kenneth
motto: "Since I first called my brother's father dad."
description: Fosness has attended St. Olaf's College and is well known as a student of West Point military academy. Martial discipline has in no way spoiled him, and he is still a prince of good fellows. Long ago he dedicated his efforts to a life of strenuous public service and aspires to the presidency of the W. C. T. U.

name: Edmonds, Oscar W.
motto: "Gentlemen can never ruffle my good nature."
description: "Foxy" first squawked at a certain burg known as Caledonia. Having chased himself for a number of years and not being able to catch up, came to the "U" in '07. His delight is in purring with a certain "Kitty," and he hopes some day to get a ride in "Kitty's" auto.

name: Power, M. John, Jr.
motto: "Though I am not splenitive and rash, Yet have I something in me dangerous."

Soon after birth he was lured to the Magic City of Dakota, and took a course in Minot schools. He makes a hit with the ladies. But for them he has no time. They are unable to appreciate his art.

name: Lynn, Harry C.
motto: "Fellows, let's go cahoots and do the public."
description: The night of Harry's birth was very dark, but some kind soul struck a light and rejoiced to see a plump, round, smiling face. That face still smiles, but Harry has acquired many habits since the day of his birth. Chief of these is a disposition to scrap with Stuart.

name: Lindstedt, Edwin
motto: "A tongue for mingled eloquence is my lot."
description: "Supposin'" is younger than he looks, having entered the College of Arts two years ago. After graduating from the high schools in Ada, Minn., he started in business in Grand Forks. He is a regular questionnaire and frequently cross-examines the profs. He is at present briefing this case in Carriers for the Dean: "If a lady falls to the pavement and you pick her up, are you a common carrier?"

name: Naplin, Oscar A.
motto: "That which ordinary men are fit for I am qualified in; and the best of me is diligence."
description: A native of Sweden, and imbued with a strong determination to do things, Oscar came to Minnesota in the early days. He received his arts degree from Gustavus Adolphus College, St. Peter, Minn. The record of his senior year at that institution speaks well for his ability. He was football captain, basketball captain and coach, as well as baseball manager.

name: Plante, Joseph Oswald
motto: "A worthy gentleman, a diffuser of the truth."
description: This distinguished looking gentleman is a hustler for degrees. He received his arts degree from the University of Manitoba '06, and he did two years post graduate work at Laval University, Montreal. Now he is after an L. L. B. from North Dakota. He is brilliant and unpretentious.

name: Sathre, P. O.
motto: "Bid me discourse, I will enchant thine ear."
description: This gentleman behind the "specs" was born at Adonis, Minn., several weeks ago. He has attended Cooperstown schools and Crookston normal school. Finding oblivion and obscurity settling upon him, he married, to make a sure thing of it, and, at the same time shatter his possibilities of ever becoming vice president of the U. S. A. Honesty and law are his ideals.

name: Swenson, John
motto: "I know what I am, but know not what I may be."
description: "John" was born on a huge corn stalk in good old Kansas, but, just as soon as he heard of a certain young lady in Dakota, he took the first prairie schooner bound for the west, landing in Mohall. Like all sons of Kansas, he is corpulent and does not care much for labor.

name: Thrall, J. Brainard Jr.
motto: "Take a hunch, my boy, and cop her."
description: There is always something doing when J. B. Jr. is around. He was born a Mormon, Salt Lake City, Utah, but early removal to his present home in the old Bay state, stemmed the development of any acute symptoms. His delight is in fetching the code for the Dean.

name: Watts, Harry A.
motto: "Some day I'll sue my wife for breach of promise."
description: He with the golden locks and the efflorescent smile hails from the Hendrick, Iowa, schools. He pleased the judge one morning when he said that his recitation in Torts was in an advanced stage of contemplation. He is working on this case: "When plaintiff's pumpkin vine intrudes into defendant's garden and yields a pumpkin, can plaintiff recover said pumpkin?"

name: Yaeck, Reinhold H.
motto: "I am for men, and women, too." —HENRY GEORGE.
description: Many moons past, this youth met his Waterloo, but it has made a man of him. He received his arts degree from Moravian College, Bethlehem, Pa. Eastern atmosphere has given him a delightful voice and his silver tones delight the ear of woman.

name: Thorpe, John C.
motto: "With my eloquence I will dissolve the jury."
description: Jack commenced to "follow the gleam" at Concordia College, Moorhead, Minn., but decided to come to the University two years ago, and entered the Arts department. Eloquence of speech aptly fits him for the legal profession. Happiness is his ultimate aim, whether single or wedded is not known.

name: Wineman, Ansel Griggs
motto: "Where's the sporting sheet, fellows?"
description: "Winie" was born young, but has somewhat recovered, and comes to class about once a week. His favorite fruit is "Olive," and his motto is, "Never too late to come to class." He attended Shattuck, where he won a reputation as a track man.
